TITLE I

GENERAL PROVISIONS

CHAPTER 1

Scope, mission and definitions

Article 5

Definitions

▼B

(1)

‘customs authorities’ means the customs administrations of the Member States responsible for applying the customs legislation and any other authority empowered under national law to apply certain customs legislation;

(2)

‘customs legislation’ means the body of legislation made up of:

(a)

the Union Customs Code and the provisions adopted at Union level supplementing or implementing it;

(b)

the Common Customs Tariff;

(c)

the legislation setting up a Union system of reliefs from customs duty;

(d)

international agreements containing customs provisions, insofar as applicable in the Union;

▼M4

(e)

provisions adopted within the framework of the common agricultural policy or of specific legislation applicable to certain goods resulting from the processing of agricultural products;

(3)

‘customs duty’ means the duty payable on the import or export of goods, as laid down in the Common Customs Tariff;

(4)

‘goods’ means any movable property, including electrical energy;

(5)

‘Union goods’ means goods falling into any of the following categories: goods wholly obtained in the customs territory of the Union; goods brought into the customs territory of the Union from countries or territories outside that territory and released for free circulation; goods obtained or produced in the customs territory of the Union from goods referred to in the second or third indents, or from a combination thereof;

(6)

‘non-Union goods’ means goods other than those referred to in point (24) of this Article or goods that have lost their Union status;

(7)

‘customs territory of the Union’ means the territory as defined in Article 4;

(8)

‘customs status’ means the status of goods as Union or non-Union goods;

(9)

‘customs declaration’ means the act whereby a person indicates, in the prescribed form and manner, a wish to place goods under a given customs procedure;

(10)

‘declarant’ means the person lodging a customs declaration, a temporary storage declaration, an entry summary declaration, an exit summary declaration or a re-export declaration in his or her own name or the person in whose name such a declaration is lodged;

(11)

‘customs representative’ means any person appointed by another person to carry out the acts and formalities required under customs legislation in dealings with the customs authorities;

(12)

‘customs procedure’ means any of the procedures laid down in Title VII under which goods may be placed;

(13)

‘customs debt’ means the obligation on a person to pay the amount of import or export duty which applies to specific goods under the customs legislation in force;

(14)

‘debtor’ means any person liable for a customs debt;

(15)

‘import duty’ means customs duty payable on the importation of goods;

(16)

‘export duty’ means customs duty payable on the exportation of goods;

(17)

'economic operator' means a person who, in the course of his or her business, is involved in activities covered by the customs legislation;

(18)

‘authorised economic operator’ means an economic operator who has been granted the status referred to in Article 38(2);

(19)

‘customs approved treatment or use’ means placing goods under a customs procedure, their entry into a free zone, their re-exportation, destruction or abandonment to the State;

(20)

‘release of goods’ means the act whereby the customs authorities make goods available for the purposes of the customs procedure for which they have been declared;

(21)

‘customs supervision’ means action taken in general by the customs authorities to ensure that customs legislation and, where appropriate, other provisions applicable to goods subject to such action are observed;

(22)

‘customs controls’ means specific acts performed by the customs authorities, to ensure compliance with customs legislation and other legislation governing the entry, exit, transit, movement, storage and end-use of goods moved between the customs territory of the Union and countries or territories outside that territory, as well as the presence and movement of non-Union goods and goods placed under the end-use procedure within the customs territory of the Union;

(23)

‘risk management’ means the systematic identification of risk and implementation of all measures necessary to limit exposure to risk;

(24)

‘presentation of goods to customs’ means the notification to the customs authorities of the arrival of goods at the customs office or at any other place designated or approved by the customs authorities and the availability of those goods for customs controls;

(25)

‘temporary storage’ means the situation in which non-Union goods are held temporarily between the time of their presentation to customs and the time when they are placed under a customs procedure or re-exported;

(26)

‘temporary storage facility’ means premises or any other place approved by the customs authorities where goods may be stored temporarily;

(27)

‘re-exportation’ means the exit from the customs territory of the Union of non-Union goods;

(28)

‘export’ means the procedure allowing Union goods to leave the customs territory of the Union;

(29)

‘outward processing’ means the procedure allowing Union goods to be exported temporarily from the customs territory of the Union for processing operations and then re-imported with total or partial relief from import duty;

(30)

‘standard exchange system’ means a procedure allowing an import good to replace an exported good;

(31)

‘inward processing’ means the procedure allowing non-Union goods to be used in the customs territory of the Union in one or more processing operations;

(32)

‘end-use’ means the procedure allowing goods to be released for free circulation, under duty exemption or at a reduced rate of import duty on account of their specific use;

(33)

‘temporary admission’ means the procedure allowing non-Union goods to be used in the customs territory of the Union with total or partial relief from import duty;

(34)

‘free zone’ means a part of the customs territory of the Union where goods brought into that part are, as regards import duties and other charges, treated as if they were not on Union customs territory;

(35)

‘customs warehousing procedure’ means the procedure allowing the storage in customs warehouses of non-Union goods or Union goods;

(36)

‘customs warehouse’ means any premises authorised by and under the supervision of the customs authorities where goods may be stored under the customs warehousing procedure;

(37)

‘Union transit procedure’ means the procedure allowing the movement of goods from one point to another within the customs territory of the Union;

(38)

‘common transit procedure’ means the procedure laid down in the Convention on a common transit procedure;

(39)

‘processing operations’ means any of the following: the working of goods, including erecting or assembling them or fitting them to other goods; the processing of goods; the destruction of goods; the repair of goods, including restoring them and putting them in order; the use of goods which are not to be found in the finished products but which allow or facilitate the production of those products, even if they are entirely or partially used up in the process;

(40)

‘finished products’ means products obtained during or resulting from processing operations and includes:

(a)

the main products which are the direct result of the processing operation;

(i)

primary processed products of the first category;

(ii)

secondary processed products of the first category;

(b)

secondary products which are by-products of the processing operation other than the main products;

(41)

‘waste and scrap’ means goods that cannot be used for the purpose for which they were manufactured, for example as a result of a processing operation;

Article 6

Electronic systems and data processing techniques

▼M4

(1)

‘electronic data interchange system’ means a system for electronic exchange of information using agreed message formats;

Article 100

(1)

‘annex term’ means a term that should be skipped;

OJ L 317, 9.12.2022, p. 1.

OJ L 269, 10.10.2013, p. 1.
